case_title: MARIANNA PRISTUPA VS. THI HOLDINGS, INC. ET AL

PROOF OF SERVICE BY U.S. MAIL OF NOTICE OF CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE, ADR INFORMATION PACKAGE NOTICE OF CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE; ALTERNATIVE DISPUTE RESOLUTION INFORMATION PACKAGE (TRANSACTION ID # 58779309) FILED BY PLAINTIFF PRISTUPA, MARIANNA

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CAL-SAVE INSURANCE SERVICES' DEMURRER TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE, IF SHE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO ALLEGE DELAYED DISCOVERY OF CAL-SAVE'S ALLEGED NEGLIGENT CONDUCT AND TO PLEAD A CLAIM FOR NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION THAT IS NOT TIME-BARRED. SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE A FORM OF ORDER THAT TRACKS THE TENTATIVE RULING PURSUANT CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: JUDGE KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

PROOF OF SERVICE OF NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO SET ASIDE DISMISSAL OF DEFENDANTS THI HOLDINGS INC AND DOES 1 - 10 MEMORANDUM IN SUPPORT THEREOF (TRANSACTION ID # 60682371) FILED BY PLAINTIFF PRISTUPA, MARIANNA

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MARIANNA PRISTUP'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DISMISSAL OF DEFENDANTS T H I HOLDINGS, INC. AND DOES 1-10 IS GRANTED. MS. PRISTUPA HAS SHOWN THAT SHE FILED THE DISMISSAL UNDER THE MISTAKEN BELIEF THAT SHE WOULD BE ABLE TO OBTAIN AND RETAIN A DEFAULT JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFENDANT CAL-SAVE. THIS CONSTITUTES EXCUSABLE MISTAKE AND SURPRISE PER CCP 473(B), NOTWITHSTANDING THAT THE DISMISSAL WAS INTENTIONAL WHEN MADE. GRANTING THE MOTION IS SUPPORTED BY CALIFORNIA'S STRONG POLICY OF DECIDING CASES ON THEIR MERITS. NOR HAS T H I SHOWN THAT IT WOULD SUFFER ANY LEGAL PREJUDICE IF THE MOTION IS GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189
DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF DEMURRER FILED BY DEFENDANT POROSHINA, NATALIA DBA CAL-SAVE INSURANCE SERVICES, INC.

PLAINTIFFS OPPOSITION TO DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 59139475) FILED BY PLAINTIFF PRISTUPA, MARIANNA

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT NATALIA POROSHINA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR BECAUSE IT APPEARS THAT THE MOVING PARTY IS A CORPORATION WHO IS REPRESENTED BY SOMEONE OTHER THAN AN ATTORNEY (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS). DEFENDANT CAL-SAVE INSURANCE SERVICES, INC. HAS UNTIL AUGUST 22, 2016 TO OBTAIN COUNSEL AND FILE A NEW RESPONSE TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. CAL-SAVE INSURANCE SERVICES, INC.'S FAILURE TO OBTAIN COUNSEL AND/OR FILE A TIMELY RESPONSE COULD RESULT IN A DEFAULT BEING ENTERED AGAINST IT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR #6576, BUS. TEL: 530-339-5023. (D302)

NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT, IF ENTERED PURSUANT TO CCP 473(B) (TRANSACTION ID # 17095001) FILED BY DEFENDANT CAL-SAVE INSURANCE SERVICES, INC. HEARING SET FOR MAY-05-2017 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302
ORDER TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND, ORDER DEFAULT OF JAN-17-2017 SET ASIDE

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CAL-SAVE INSURANCE SERVICES, INC.'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. CAL-SAVE'S MOTION PAPERS MAKE CLEAR THAT IT IS SEEKING RELIEF PER THE MANDATORY RELIEF PROVISIONS OF CCP 473(B) AND IT HAS SHOWN THAT IT IS ENTITLED TO SUCH RELIEF. CAL-SAVE MUST FILE A RESPONSE TO THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT NO LATER THAN MAY 18, 2017. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR #8189 =302/HK

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MARIANNA PRISTUPA'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 473B IS GRANTED IN SMALL PART. MS. PRISTUPA IS AWARDED REASONABLE FEES AND COSTS OF $430 AGAINST DEFENDANT CAL-SAVE INSURANCE SERVICES, INC. FOR MS. PRISTUPA'S PREPARATION AND FILING OF THE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT PAPERWORK. A REASONABLE HOURLY RATE FOR COUNSEL FOR MS. PRISTUPA IS $200. MS. PRISTUPA'S COUNSEL REASONABLY SPENT 2 HOURS OF TIME IN PREPARING AND FILING THE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT PAPERWORK AND INCURRED $30 IN COSTS FOR THOSE TASKS. ALL OTHER FEES AND COSTS SOUGHT BY MS. PRISTUPA ARE NOT COMPENSABLE. NO ADDITIONAL WORK WAS WARRANTED. THERE WAS NO REASONABLE BASIS TO OPPOSE THE MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CAL-SAVE INSURANCE SERVICES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION IN PLAINTIFF MARIANNA PRISTUPA'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES SUFFICIENT FACTS AT PARAGRAPHS 37-49 OF THE TO INVOKE THE DELAYED DISCOVERY RULE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CAL-SAVE INSURANCE SERVICES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION IN THE AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT CONFIRMED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. WHILE IT APPEARS THAT PLAINTIFF MARIANNA PRISTUPA WILL BE ABLE TO ADEQUATELY ALLEGE DELAYED DISCOVERY, SHE HAS NOT DONE SO. PLAINTIFF HAS NEITHER ALLEGED THE DATES OF THE THREE EVENTS WHICH SHE ALLEGES IN PARAGRAPH 50 WHICH SHE SAYS FORM THE BASIS OF HER DELAYED DISCOVERY NOR HAS SHE ADEQUATELY ALLEGED WHY SHE WAS NOT ABLE TO DISCOVER CAL-SAVE'S ALLEGED NEGLIGENCE AT AN EARLIER DATE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976.

MASTER CALENDAR COURT

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Aug-01-2017 Vacated

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DISMISSAL
Off Calendar Jun-29-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF MARIANNA PRISTUP'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DISMISSAL of defendants T H I Holdings, Inc. and Does 1-10 is GRANTED. Ms. Pristupa has shown that she filed the dismissal under the mistaken belief that she would be able to obtain and retain a default judgment against defendant Cal-Save. This constitutes excusable mistake and surprise per CCP 473(b), notwithstanding that the dismissal was intentional when made. Granting the motion is supported by California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits. Nor has T H I shown that it would suffer any legal prejudice if the motion is granted. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 =302/hk

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-14-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. AARON BLUM, TEL. NO. 1-307-690-7286 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. BRIAN TURNER OF THE TURNER/MODARELLI, TEL. NO. 1-916-900-6845 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CAL-SAVE INSURANCE SERVICES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT as to the fourth cause of action in plaintiff Marianna Pristupa's second amended complaint is OVERRULED. Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts at paragraphs 37-49 of the to invoke the delayed discovery rule. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 =302/hk

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Plaintiff Marianna Pristupa For Attorneys Fees And Costs Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 473b Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Jun-08-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF MARIANNA PRISTUPA'S Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 473b is GRANTED in small part. Ms. Pristupa is awarded reasonable fees and costs of $430 against defendant Cal-Save Insurance Services, Inc. for Ms. Pristupa's preparation and filing of the default and default judgment paperwork. A reasonable hourly rate for counsel for Ms. Pristupa is $200. Ms. Pristupa's counsel reasonably spent 2 hours of time in preparing and filing the default and default judgment paperwork and incurred $30 in costs for those tasks. All other fees and costs sought by Ms. Pristupa are not compensable. No additional work was warranted. There was no reasonable basis to oppose the motion to set aside the entry of default and default judgment. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 =302/hk

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment, If Entered Pursuant To Ccp 473(B)
Off Calendar May-05-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. AARON BLUM, TEL. NO. 1-307-690-7286 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. BRIAN TURNER, TEL. NO. 1-916-900-6845 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Cal-Save Insurance Services, Inc.'s motion to set aside default and default judgment is granted. Cal-Save's motion papers make clear that it is seeking relief per the mandatory relief provisions of CCP 473(b) and it has shown that it is entitled to such relief. Cal-Save must file a response to the second amended complaint no later than May 18, 2017. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-21-2016 Continued To Jan-11-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-12-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CAL-SAVE INSURANCE SERVICES, INC.'S DEMURRER to the fourth cause of action in the amended complaint is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. While it appears that plaintiff Marianna Pristupa will be able to adequately allege delayed discovery, she has not done so. Plaintiff has neither alleged the dates of the three events which she alleges in paragraph 50 which she says form the basis of her delayed discovery nor has she adequately alleged why she was not able to discover Cal-Save's alleged negligence at an earlier date. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-18-2016 Continued To Sep-14-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-20-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT NATALIA POROSHINA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is off calendar because it appears that the moving party is a corporation who is represented by someone other than an attorney. According to the California Secretary of State's database, Cal-Save Insurance Services, Inc. is a corporation. The suffix "Inc." in its name supports that conclusion as does page 12 of the points and authorities in support of the demurrer, which lists Ms. Poroshina as the president and CEO of that corporation. The California State Bar website indicates that Ms. Poroshina is not a licensed attorney. A corporation cannot represent itself through an individual that is not licensed to practice law. (Merco Constr. Engineers, Inc. v. Municipal Crt. (1978) 21 Cal.3d 724.) Defendant Cal-Save Insurance Services, Inc. has until August 22, 2016 to obtain counsel and file a new response to the first amended complaint. Cal-Save Insurance Services, Inc.'s failure to obtain counsel and/or file a timely response could result in a default being entered against it.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR #6576, Bus. Tel: 530-339-5023. (D302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-23-2016 Continued To Aug-03-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-04-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Attorney Aaron Blum telephone number 307-690-7286 for Marianna Pristupa.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendant Cal-Save Insurance Services' demurrer to the fourth cause of action for negligence is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. Plaintiff is granted leave, if she can do so in good faith, to allege delayed discovery of Cal-Save's alleged negligent conduct and to plead a claim for negligent misrepresentation that is not time-barred. Sanctions are denied. The prevailing party is to provide a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling pursuant CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
